reviews.title: The Song Nightmare!
reviews.text:
Prior to coming to the Sóng Apartment, I sent a message asking about accessing to which I got no reply for 2 days.  Upon arriving, I was confused as there were several Blocks of apartment.  Phone several times and NO ONE PICKED UP THE PHONE!!!  Asked security personnel to which ị got nowhere as he didn’t know.   I took a chance and went to another older man and he gave direction.  By the time I passed the 2nd block building, I knew that this wasn’t going to be an easy check-in because there was no map, no response from whomever rented this place to guests like me.

There was a small hot room when I stumble upon it and seeing a young girl with a high school notebook and she had guests name in them.  My name wasn’t in it even after i gave my passport she was still looking for something else but didn’t tell me what it was… confused, I ask.  What are you looking for???  She reply:  You’re NOT on the guest list or register and I responded - YES I AM, HERE’S THE PROOF OF MY CREDIT CARD PAYMENT, EMAIL, AND SCREENSHOT OF ALL MY BOOKINGS!!!  What else are you looking for and she told me she needed my “proof of citizenship!”  I thought this was the most bizarre request I’ve ever had.  Usually the Passport was enough as I’m a Canadian citizen NOT a Vietnamese citizen!  My wife think she’s looking for the Visa (Miễn Thị Thực) which is a light blue booklet issue when one enter a communist country.  I gave her ours and she flipped through it looking for the date of entry.  Once that was recorded, we were told to put a 200k vnd deposit, a small plastic bag filled with small bags of cheap ass  shampoo and body wash and a disgusting hairdryer which we would not even used.  

We were directed to go to another building and this was where the bad experience began… 4 elevators.  We waited for 10 minutes just to get on one of them which was the nearest to the entrance.  It stopped at every single floor.  Ours was on the 7th floor and if anyone decides to book this apartment, DON’T BOOK HIGHER THAN THE 20TH FLOOR or you are so screwed because the wait will be horrendous as there are 36 floors.  Also, the maximum any fire fighting ladder trucks can go is 20 floors which is 200 feet… any higher than that and the ladders will collapse due to the weight of it.

We finally found our room.  Upon entering, we had to orient ourselves as none of the lights worked nor AC.  I pulled out my head lamp looking for the electrical panel and was able to flip the switch ON.  We checked the bed and it was very uncomfortable almost as hard as a rock to lie on.  My wife found several strands of hair from previous guest on our bed… EWWWWW!!!!

The ocean view was false, we got to stare at other tenants.  The Gym that was posted ya had to stay a minimum of 30 days before you can access it!!  At this point we knew we had been SCAM!!!!  Nothing of what we saw of this apartment nor the requested information was even given to us when we needed help.  My wife says,… we are booking another hotel, a real one, not some BS vacation condo/apartment that doesn’t live up to its name.  I that point i was so tired… in the middle of the night we woke up to the sound of siren and a PA system with someone announcing that there was an emergency of some sort in the hallway evacuating everyone and be prepared to pack and leave as soon as a second investigation and an announcement will be made.  I looked outside to the opposite side of the other building.  People were up and confuse, daze from the abrupt siren… to make a long story short.  We booked 4 nights but only slept one night there.  My legs fell asleep and each time i wake up when turning to my side just to try and keep the blood flowing.

The Knife in the kitchen is rusted, the fridge handle is broken, the curtains and some broken parts to it, the toilet leak at the base so when you flush, water would also flow outward at night.  there was also tiny little ants crawling all over the dining table, kitchen and some even made it’s way in

I booked  2 nights but after one night I had to book another hotel to stay the second night.   It is very poor quality service.   There is no hot water.    After waited for a very long time to have somebody to turn on the tank.    But the next morning, there is no hot water again.    I called them but still no promptly service.  They don’t get the room ready for customer.     There is no power in the room Until somebody enter my room to turn it on.   The elevator is crowded with residence.  It took forever to go up/downstairs.   
I requested to get refund for the second night because of their fault, but they refused.     It is a very bad place.  They use very low quality for beddings materials     Stay away from this apartment.    It’s not worth it for your time and money.

reviews.title: Very terrible
reviews.text:
It's the first time I've stayed in such a terrible room. First of all, he didn't tell me how to check in before check-in. After I arrived, he called me to add WhatsApp, but he didn't reply after adding it. It was not until I found the apartment manager that I found their office at the end of a small door on the first floor of Building A.
Secondly, the supplies are very poor. I don't understand. The room that costs *** VND for two nights is not cheap, but there is no free water, the shampoo is of poor quality, and the quality of the towels and bath towels is also very poor, not to mention the toilet paper provided. I have never used such a bad one.
Finally, the environment is not good. One of the small bedrooms has no window and no air conditioner, so only an electric fan can be used for heating. And there are very few elevators in the building I live in. It's no exaggeration to say that I live on the 14th floor. I went downstairs and waited for the elevator for half an hour. And we didn't use the kitchen. There was a strange smell in the refrigerator, and there were small reptiles on the kitchen table. It's a very bad residence, and it's not recommended to live in.

reviews.title: Acceptable
reviews.text:
We booked an apartment with separate bedromm. On arrival around 15:00 our bedroom wasn't ready.
The host gave us a studio apartment that wasn't what we booked. 
We accept as they didn't have our booked apartment. They said tomorrow they give us our booked room. The next day they showed us one bedroom apartment which wasn't our booked one. Then we stayed in the studio as it looked like the best what they have had.
The studio apartment cleanness was under the hospitality standard. We ask the host for cleaning that happened every 3ŕd day. Cleaning took about 5 minutes, only flooring ans surface cleaning. We ask to clean spider web all around ceilng and in every corner very visible. The lady clear said she doesn't have long stick broom to rich the hight of the ceiling.
The spider web is at least there for last 2,3 months.  Bathroom tiles corners full of mould everywhere. 
Balcony corners lots of cigarettes buts. No balcony table and chairs as advertised. The kitchen sink very dirty,  no sponge washing dishes, halt cutlery missing, frying pans dirty and from food burns on the inside frying pans. Only on porcelain plate and one plastic plate and a few bowls 🥣 
Bedlinen was changed only ones in 6 nights. TV doesn't have antena or internet channels. You need to use your own internet pasward access for  any other option to watch normal Vietnamese ir international channels.
The host that represents many of the apartment owners is unexpirienced in managing customer service. 
However,  the position of the property is walking dinstance to lote-mart, which is for shoping convenience very handy. A short walk to the beach and beautiful walking pats.
Thanks to Vietnamese government the beach, walking pats, bikes pats for transformation of the public beach.
In a couple of years, one's the Palms, park gardens and beach infrastructures are finish with all renovations it would be WORLD CLASS beach. This was our third stay in Vung Tau in the last 4 years.
In general it wasn't bad but accommodation was want satisfactory this time.
I hope that this is only our sugestion and not a complaint

reviews.title: Không nên đi lại
reviews.text: Đầu năm không muốn kể xấu nhưng mà phải nói sự thật để mọi người cân nhắc lựa chọn cho đúng, việc đầu tiên không hài lòng đó là nhắn tin trao đổi thì kiệm lời, không hỗ trợ khách đổi ngày dù là có tình huống bệnh bất khả kháng, sau mình vẫn ráng đi thì vừa tới nơi thì nhân viên (mình nghĩ là chị chủ) phụ thu thêm 200k/đêm vì nói là có 2 trẻ con, trong khi phòng ghi là miễn phí trẻ con dưới 5t, nhà mình có 2 bé 1 bé 3 tuổi, 1 bé 5 tuổi nhưng vẫn bị phụ thu.   Việc thứ 2: bước vào phòng tuy không có mùi hôi như 1 số đánh giá, nhưng sàn thì nhớt nhợt trơn trợt (chắc lau bằng xà phòng hay gì), mà không có lấy 1 đôi dép mang trong phòng nên phải tự đi mua dép trong nhà để mang. Việc thứ 3: căn hộ 2 phòng ngủ nhưng cái phòng ngủ  phụ thì bé xíu chừng 6m2 không có máy lạnh mà chỉ có cái quạt, quạt thì không cắm được dây, sau phải lấy cái quạt bên ngoài vô để chật cái phòng không còn lối đi luôn, ngủ cũng không dám đóng cửa vì dùng chung máy lạnh phòng khách, ai ra vô ồn đành chịu. Mình nghĩ đây là căn hộ 1 phòng ngủ nhưng chủ nhà tự ngăn đôi, vì phòng ngủ chính cũng bé xíu xiu. Việc thứ 4: thang máy chờ lâu muốn xỉu, nhà mình không tắm biển chứ tắm chắc chờ lạnh bệnh luôn, đặc biệt vào giờ check in- check out, chờ gần 1 tiếng vì có vali nên kẹt thang nhỏ xíu mà vài chục tầng nên lúc nào cũng full, sau phải tách ra mỗi người đi 1 lượt riêng mới đi được. May là mình đi vào ngày thường trước lễ không phải cao điểm trong lễ  mà còn vậy. Cái được duy nhất là toà nhà có GS25 bán 24/7 nên ăn vặt dễ và gần mấy khách sạn nên ăn trong mấy nhà hàng đó tạm ổn. Với giá 900k/đêm mà chưa gồm ăn sáng  thì nên ở bên DIC Landmark kế bên sạch sẽ phòng to rộng, giường êm ái chứ không phải cái nệm gối như nhà nghỉ, ra vô không có ai tiếp đón xách đồ, mượn gì là phải tự chạy xuống lấy tự phục vụ như vầy, cô chủ nói bên này có bếp khác bển thì mình thấy không khác gì  hết chỉ có tệ hơn thôi nha vì họ cũng có bếp dạng căn hộ mà phòng thì siêu to (nhà mình tuy không nấu nướng gì không cần, năm nay tưởng bên này mới thử đổi gió thì đúng là quá sai)

reviews.title: a lot to improve on
reviews.text: I had high hopes hopes for this. reviews seemed ok so I went for it. its not overall bad. but there were so many negatives that im left to be as honest as possible about it. the main one is the towels. they give it to you upon check, in a plastic bag with a hair dryer and some satchels of shampoo and body wash. so weird. it gives a budget/cheap feel. The towels smelled, one smelled like body odour, the other had no bad smell but smelled old. they are not washed well or maybe they ran out of laundry powder. and just rinsed by water and dried. I had to buy another towel because there was no reply from the WhatsApp chat. the satchels of shampoo and body wash is so meh. its so hard to open, I had to get a knife from the kitchen to open them. tip bring your own stuff. the kettle in the room did not work, I went down to the office and the guy gave me the kettle in his office. he emptied the water and just gave it to me.  the room description says luxury oceanfront suite, but its far from oceanfront. you can barely see anything and not as pictured. there is a giant building in front of you as pictured attached. next the staff sux. the short guy who was our contact never replied ever. I dont know if it's him on the other end or the owner of the condo, but neither replied. so poor communication, you are left on your own. the location is ok, theres not much to do by the beach. there is no beach front eateries. more local stuff. you need to go a little further in to find proper food. outside the condo is ok, its shaded and a lot of places to sit and chill with some nice breeze. besides that, nothing much positive about it. they dont even have a pool, I tried to ask, but again no reply. I wouldn't go to this one at list not this listing. maybe try the other listings on this building. save yourself the trouble.

reviews.title: DỊCH VỤ & TIỆN NGHI
reviews.text:
Kính gửi Đội ngũ quản lý Trinh’s House,
Mình viết thư này để phản ánh ánh sáng về trải nghiệm lưu trú tại phòng *** vào ngày 1.7.2025
Khi nhận phòng, mình thực sự ấn tượng với không gian sạch sẽ, thoáng mát và tầm nhìn đẹp . Tuy nhiên, trong quá trình sử dụng, mình đã gặp phải một số vấn đề về thiếu chuyên nghiệp trong dịch vụ và tiện nghi, cụ thể như sau:
* Missing in setting phòng tắm:
    * Các công cụ bảo vệ cá nhân (như khăn tắm) không được chuẩn bị sẵn trong phòng mà yêu cầu bọn mình mang lên. Điều này mình thật sự cả thấy rất. khó chịu do chúng mình vừa trải qua một chặng đường dài từ SG lên VT và chiếc khăn tắm màu trắng được cung cấp lại nhiều vết nhơ, gây cảm giác không sẽ và thiếu vệ sinh.
* Vấn đề vệ sinh và tiện nghi phòng tắm:
    * Gương nhà tắm còn nhiều vệt nhơ
    * Bồn cầu còn dấu vàng, cho thấy việc vệ sinh chưa kỹ lưỡng.
    * Không có dép đi trong nhà, trong khi sàn nhà chưa được lau chùi sạch sẽ(đi rất khó chịu chân)
* Thiết bị trong phòng không hoạt động:
        * TV và Wifi không hoạt động, ảnh hưởng đến nhu cầu giải trí và kết nối của tụi mình 
    * Tủ lạnh không làm lạnh thậm chí mở ra lại còn hơi nóng 
Những vấn đề trên, dù nhỏ, đã ảnh hưởng đáng kể đến trải nghiệm và sự thoải mái của mình trong thời gian lưu trú. Là một khách hàng, mình mong đợi được chuẩn bị chu đáo và chất lượng dịch vụ đồng thời tốt nhất từ một cơ sở lưu trữ.
Mình hy vọng những lời khuyên này sẽ giúp Trinh's House Apartment cải thiện chất lượng dịch vụ để mang lại trải nghiệm tốt hơn cho các khách hàng khác trong tương lai.
Xin chân thành cảm ơn quý khách sạn đã lắng nghe phản hồi của mình.
Trân trọng.

reviews.title: Khách đông nhưng ít nhân viên, chờ dọn phòng lâu
reviews.text:
Vì thấy nhiều lượt đánh giá nên mình chọn, có lẽ vì đông khách mà chất lượng phục vụ không ok.
Ban đầu mình đặt 1 căn 3 phòng ngủ trên app, sau đó nhắn zalo c chủ bảo là hết căn đó rồi nên giao cho mình 2 căn mỗi căn 2pn cạnh nhau tổng là 4pn, mình vẫn vui vẻ đồng ý (mặc dù nếu ban đầu đặt 2 căn như v giá rẻ hơn 1 căn 3pn)
Thế nhưng lúc đến check-in thì chị chủ giao cho mình 1 căn 2pn và 1 căn 1pn với lí do là ban đầu mình đặt 1 căn chỉ có 3pn thôi, mình kiểu wtf?? trong khi sự thật là 1 căn 3 pn giá cao hơn và rộng rãi hơn, ko thể đánh đồng với 1 căn 2pn và 1 căn 1pn diện tích nhỏ hơn nhiều như kia được => Cái mình ko hài lòng ở đây là chị chủ nói chuyện lúc này lúc khác
Thời gian đợi nhận phòng rất lâu, phải đến 14h30 mới dọn phòng xong để cho mình vào check-in, có lẽ do ít nhân viên, không giống như khách sạn, cuối tuần thì quá đông khách
Lúc nhận phòng cũng chưa có khăn liền, mình phải quay lại sau đó để lấy

reviews.text:
Pro - large airy apt with kitchen utensils and washing machine. Nice pool and gym. 
Con - not clean. Linen, towels, kitchen, untensils. The microwave in the apt was not working and the staff was very quick to replace it but the replacement was smelly and dirty from the previous user. There were cockroaches in the bathroom, the staff was quick to spray something down but I still trapped and killed one.
Pool was impossible to use as it was overrun by hooligans, swearing, bad mouthing language teenagers and people without pool etiquette. The elevators were not sufficient to accommodate all the population. 
It’s value for money if you can accept the conditions described.

reviews.title: Crowded and smelly building
reviews.text: This building seemed popular for local tourists. There were a lot of family and children in the building.  The stink foods could be smelled in the hallways. The elevators  not efficient. It took 10 to 15 minutes to wait for an elevator ride. The room was spacious but poorly maintained. It looked dirty and smelly upon check-in. The property staff was responsive to complaints but they couldn’t do much to improve it. The bed was uncomfortable. The bedsheets and towels didn’t look very clean and made of cheap materials. There were ants running all over the place. The beach is within walking distance but there were not many restaurants and entertainment venues nearby. There’s a Lotte shopping center across the street. I wouldn’t stay in this building again.

reviews.title: It's alright.
reviews.text: This was an impromptu trip and I booked it quickly at night, and the pictures on the app compared to reality left me a bit disappointed. If I book through the app at that price, I shouldn't expect too much because I could have paid more, but the room images and price on the app were my deciding factors. I rode my motorbike from Saigon to Vung Tau, so I had already informed them, but when I arrived, I had to call 2 or 3 times before they answered because they were already asleep :((( Upon entering the room, there was a foul smell, the bathroom lacked basic supplies, and there was no dryer or anything. The floor was dirty, the fridge had ants due to not being cleaned properly, which made me really uneasy. The sofa was dirty, and the table next to the sofa was greasy and sticky. The switches worked intermittently; touching them made them fall off. This is the first time I've had such a bad experience.

reviews.title: A bit disappointing.
reviews.text: The room was fairly clean, but the mattress, blankets, pillows, and the sofa cover all smelled used and had not been replaced. The room had a washing machine and dryer, but it seemed like it hadn't been cleaned for a long time, so the clothes came out smelling unpleasant. Despite renting the room, guests were not provided with bottled water, and the toilet paper was of cheap quality. The kitchen had a decent amount of utensils but was a bit cluttered: chopsticks were mismatched, there wasn't a complete pair, the kettle had a broken lid yet still worked, and the dish soap was kept in a bottled water container instead of a proper dish soap bottle. The small bedroom only had a fan, while only the living room and the large bedroom had air conditioning, so to stay cool, you had to turn on the air conditioning from the living room and open the bedroom door. I stayed during the week and still had to wait quite a long time for the elevator, which was quite crowded. The advantage is that it is close to the beach and has a kitchen for cooking. Basically, you get what you pay for; it only reaches this level, and I will not return or recommend anyone to stay here.

reviews.title: Family-friendly
reviews.text: Trinh's house apartment is fine. Upon arrival, I called their phone and was directed to the B lobby to ask the receptionist to find Trinh's house cultural area inside on the ground floor. There, I was given an elevator card that included a password to the apartment. I had to leave an ID as a deposit. My apartment is 3307 on the 33rd floor with an internal view and a view of a distant corner of the sea. The apartment is small and cute, just enough for 3 people. Quite clean. There is a washing machine, but you have to bring your own laundry powder/liquid. Unfortunately, there are no indoor slippers; if needed, you have to buy them at the office for 20k/pair. Water in the fridge is charged. The colorful bath towels are not very nice. Everyone should bring their own towels, shower gel, shampoo, etc. if they want everything to be complete and clean. The balcony has a place to dry clothes, but there are no chairs on the balcony like some other neighboring apartments 😆 900k/night on weekends is very acceptable. It’s a 5-minute walk to the beach, and for meals, just grab a ride.

reviews.title: Traveling as if living.
reviews.text:
I was confused upon arrival and didn't know what to do (check-in is required at TRINH's HOUSE office in room 1-21 after entering from entrance C. It's strange that this information wasn't provided in advance).

It's so-called serviced apartment, and the room is spacious and comfortable.
You can watch Netflix on the smart TV.
There is a drum washing machine (no detergent provided, and instructions are only in Vietnamese).
You can cook instant noodles in the kitchen.
There is no microwave.
Only bath towels are provided.
I stayed for 5 nights, but there was no cleaning or towel change during my stay.
Since it was on the 3rd floor, I wanted to use the stairs, but it felt closed off and scary.
City view = right in front is a building under construction, but if you go out to the balcony, you can see the sea in the distance.
There is a convenience store GS25 and a cafe on the 1st floor, and Lotte Mart is nearby, which is convenient.
However, I couldn't find any nice restaurants.

Perhaps they are targeting Vietnamese people, but as a foreigner, I would have liked more explanations about various things.
The staff is not fluent in English but was friendly.

reviews.title: Very satisfied!
reviews.text: My family consists of 2 adults, 2 children aged 14, a 6-year-old, and a 2-year-old staying in a 2-bedroom apartment with a sofa bed, which is just enough. The apartment is fully equipped with 2 air conditioners, and a washing machine is essential because my family loves to swim at the beach every day. The staff is very helpful, and there are housekeeping services (not dishwashing) when I called, a refrigerator, electric stove, and electric kettle. Overall, it's comfortable like being at home. I find it very convenient to stay in an apartment with small kids. There’s a separate pool for adults and children and a play area for the little ones. The prices are also quite reasonable. I went at the beginning of summer, in June, and the price was around 600-700k, I forgot exactly. I will definitely come back when I visit Vung Tau and recommend it to friends and family to experience. I stayed at Trinh’s house. The location is near Lotte supermarket and close to the center.

reviews.title: Satisfactory - Happy
reviews.text: I booked this Trinh's House apartment 3 nights in advance, and tonight is an additional night because my child doesn't want to go home yet. The owner was very supportive, even though I rebooked a room with one bed, she still allowed me to stay in the 2-bedroom apartment because I had already stayed for 3 days. Since it's not the peak tourist season right now, the elevator congestion is not significant. In general, if anyone is traveling with a large group, it's better to book an apartment for convenience in daily activities.

reviews.title: Experience at The Sóng
reviews.text:
I went with 2 people for 1 night.  
Pros:  
+ Modern and fully equipped interior furnishings from Addeens Z, suitable for those who enjoy cooking.  
+ Close to Lotte Mart, so you can park there for free.  
+ Easy to move to the central districts of the city (districts 1, 2, 3).  
+ I went during the week, so I didn't have to wait long for the elevator.  
+ You can leave your bags early if you arrive ahead of time.  
Cons:  
+ The owner rarely replies to messages, so you have to call directly.  
+ A bit far from the beach, so walking may be a bit tough for some.  
+ The towels have some yellow stains and are not very clean, but if requested, the owner will assist with a change.  
+ I stayed on the 12th floor, so the view mainly faced Lotte Mart, and there's nothing particularly nice to see.  
+ The parking fee is a bit high, so make sure to inquire about parking fees in advance, or in the morning, you can park at Lotte Mart for free.

reviews.title: Highly recommend
reviews.text:
During my recent trip to Vũng Tàu, I had the opportunity to stay at The Sóng Apartment – Trinh’s House, and overall, it turned out to be a highly satisfying experience. The apartment offers an impressive combination of affordability, convenience, and comfort, making it a worthwhile choice for travellers seeking a relaxing getaway.

One of the apartment’s most outstanding features is its excellent location. It is situated close to the beach and several popular attractions, which made it extremely easy to explore the city without spending too much time on transportation. The facilities and amenities inside the building were also commendable. The room was modern, well-designed, and fully equipped, providing everything I needed for a comfortable stay. Additionally, the level of cleanliness was remarkable; the apartment was spotless from the moment I arrived. In terms of safety, the building maintains a high standard of security, which further contributed to my peace of mind throughout the trip.

However, despite these strengths, there was one area that fell short of my expectations: the attitude of the staff. While they fulfilled their basic responsibilities, they appeared somewhat unenthusiastic and lacked the friendliness typically expected in the hospitality industry. A warmer and more welcoming approach would significantly enhance the overall guest experience.

In conclusion, The Sóng Apartment – Trinh’s House is a well-priced and well-located accommodation option with impressive facilities and strong security measures. Although there is room for improvement in customer service, the apartment remains a solid choice for visitors to Vũng Tàu, and I would still consider staying here again in the future.

reviews.title: 3-day 2-night trip
reviews.text: This is my first time staying at The Sóng. I have researched very thoroughly and chose to book a room with Ms. Trinh. My first impression of being here is quite good, and the price I booked on Agoda has both pros and cons. Firstly, I would like to point out a few issues: if you book a room with Ms. Trinh, you should contact her via the phone number provided on Agoda at least 2-3 hours before check-in so she can arrange accordingly. I see she is quite busy, so please be understanding of that. Secondly, I want to criticize the TV in the room; the sound is distorted and it’s hard to listen to music, which really needs to be fixed. Thirdly, regarding the utensils, I find that the spoons and chopsticks are somewhat worn out (one pair of chopsticks and a few spoons, but they look a bit dirty), so if you stay here, either eat out or if you bring food back to the room, remember to buy disposable utensils. Now for the compliments: the room has a pretty nice view. I stayed on the 12th floor, room 15, and from the balcony, there is a corner view of the sea, which is great for pictures, although directly in front are two buildings under construction. Another compliment is that when I entered the room, the staff member was very friendly and showed me where the light switch was. When we checked in, she had just finished cleaning the room, which smelled fresh and was clean. The balcony glass is dirty, but I understand that it’s hard to clean. Other than that, everything is very good; there is a washing machine, so if you stay for a long time, you can do laundry. Additionally, if you go to the infinity pool on the 36th floor, remember to bring swimwear because you need it to go down and you cannot use cameras. Make sure to go early since the elevator can get very crowded; I spent an hour getting down to the ground floor. Other than that, everything is wonderful.

reviews.title: End-of-year travel 2024 - Western New Year
reviews.text: Convenient location - easy to find near 3/2 street in Vung Tau city. The friendly host provides a key card for access to the room after confirming the booking at The Sóng lobby. The room is clean, airy, with a view overlooking the entire Vung Tau and some sea view. The downside is that when I arrived, the bathroom was without towels, shower gel, and hand soap, so everyone should keep that in mind (it could also be that the host was too busy to prepare in time). The kitchen space is moderate, not too spacious, suitable for 2-3 people to eat. When I arrived, the kitchen hood could not be turned on! Overall, it is clean, well-equipped, and the soundproofing around the rooms is quite good! I will come back!

reviews.title: Review for the Nth time this year.
reviews.text:
This month I am so into The Sóng that I’m coming back for the second time 😆. The apartment is very clean, fully equipped, and just about 100 meters away from the beach. The rooftop infinity pool is still 'top-notch', going up there to enjoy the sky and clouds is both relaxing and classy.

There's something a bit funny: for anyone driving, remember to avoid the area near the school next door, when it rains the water can get quite high, be careful or your vehicle might end up like a... 'submarine' 🤣.

Overall, staying at The Sóng is both comfortable and convenient, I've tried it twice this month and I'm not bored yet. I'm sure there will be more times, maybe a third time too 🏖️.

reviews.title: A great deal apartment in the seaside city.
reviews.text: I just had a wonderful experience at The Sóng apartment. The room is clean, modernly designed, and fully equipped with amenities from the kitchen, washing machine to a cool balcony overlooking the city. The infinity pool on the top floor is very beautiful, and the view at night is extremely chill. The location is convenient, close to the beach and downtown, making it easy to go anywhere. The host/staff are very supportive, and the check-in process is quick. With a very reasonable price, this is a great deal for a trip to Vung Tau. I will definitely come back and recommend it to my friends.

reviews.title: Exceptional
reviews.text:
This apartment complex was recommended by a local friend. I secured a very good price booking online. The complex has more than a few eateries and a convenience store. You have gym, sauna, and pool access. Perhaps a hair salon and a massage centre. I did not do a full check. There is street food outside till late, and scooter hire. I can honestly say nothing bad about the accommodation and complex beyond waiting for elevators. It took up to an hour to check in as the foyer was full of people checking in as well. 

There was some confusion entering the apartment as the display panel was not fully displayed to enter the door code. Put your hand over the display panel to show full keypad, then type in your door code. When leaving, cover display panel with your hand to lock.

Wear a mask in the underground car park. You have to pay, but the cost is around $1.50 AUD per day. Take a screenshot to find your bike. I got lost. Shit happens. Highly recommended,  if you get a good price. I paid AUD 106 for 4 nights

reviews.title: Exceptional
reviews.text: The room is okay but you should find a way to fix the drain in the toilet; when I first checked in, the drain smelled really bad. I stayed for a few days and kept the exhaust fan on so it was better. Everything else is fine, it's airy and not too hot, and the bedding is clean.

reviews.title: The wave
reviews.text: The room was quite clean, with full amenities. I used the elevator in the middle of the week, so I didn't have to wait long. The owner was quite enthusiastic; the only downside was that there were no indoor slippers and no towels. Overall, it was a pretty good experience.
